tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy or acute metabolic decompensation. Alkaline phosphatase is mildly elevated, and urine is abnormal in appearance and color. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic control, and possible underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
